reasons: Diggie can protect Claude during Blazing Duet so Claude is less likely to be stopped by normal crowd control.
Claude benefits from Diggie's shield and control immunity during his highest-risk teamfight entry.

proof.summary: Claude wants a safe AoE entry window, while Diggie reduces crowd-control interruption during that window.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves Time Journey for Claude's real commit.
Claude enters after enemy control has been baited.
failureCases: Diggie uses Time Journey before Claude commits.

reasons: Diggie can protect Irithel from crowd-control engage while she keeps moving and attacking.
Irithel benefits from Diggie's protection because her damage depends on uninterrupted spacing.

proof.summary: Irithel needs uninterrupted spacing, while Diggie protects her from the crowd-control window that would normally stop her kiting.
worksBestWhen: Diggie stays close enough to Irithel before enemy engage.
Irithel keeps moving behind the protected zone.
failureCases: Irithel is caught outside Time Journey range.
Enemy uses suppression or delayed control after Time Journey ends.
Diggie is forced to use protection for himself first.

reasons: Diggie can protect Odette from normal crowd-control interruption during her AoE damage window.
Odette benefits when Diggie times protection after enemies commit to stopping her channel.

proof.summary: Odette needs time to apply AoE damage, while Diggie can protect her from normal control during the key window.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves Time Journey for Odette's real cast.
Odette positions inside Diggie's protection range.
failureCases: Enemy suppression or displacement bypasses the protection plan.
Odette channels too far from Diggie.
Diggie uses Time Journey before Odette is threatened.

reasons: Diggie can protect Layla's immobile backline damage pattern from crowd-control engage.
Layla benefits when Diggie prevents one engage from immediately ending her damage window.

proof.summary: Layla needs space and safety, while Diggie helps prevent crowd-control engage from immediately ending her damage window.
worksBestWhen: Diggie positions near Layla before enemy tanks engage.
failureCases: Layla is flanked from outside Diggie's position.
Diggie is baited into using Time Journey early.
Enemy burst kills Layla after the protection duration ends.

reasons: Diggie can add protection to Hanabi's teamfight damage pattern when enemy engage relies on normal crowd control.
Hanabi benefits from staying inside Diggie's protected formation while dealing backline damage.

proof.summary: Hanabi wants to stand and output in teamfights, while Diggie helps cover her against crowd-control engage.
worksBestWhen: Diggie times Time Journey after the enemy commits.
Hanabi stays near the protected team formation.
failureCases: Enemy suppresses or bursts after Time Journey ends.
Diggie uses protection before the enemy poke or dive arrives.
